anchor.quote: keep the first MCTS/search implementation in Python
anchor.originalQuote: Should MCTS live entirely in Python at first, or move the hot tree into Rust once the Python shape is proven?
anchor.prefix: implement simple deterministic one-ply or depth-limited search first
anchor.suffix: move hot search loops into Rust only later

thread.author: user
thread.body: Let's leave it in Python. We can figure out whether we need to move it into Rust later, with benchmarks.

thread.author: codex
thread.body: Agreed. I changed Slice 2 so the first MCTS/search implementation stays in Python, and Rust only becomes an option later if benchmarks show Python is too slow.

anchor.quote: Which combat fixture should be the first UI/search demo? Examples:
anchor.originalQuote: Which combat fixture should be the first UI/search demo?

thread.author: user
thread.body: What does this mean? add examples please.

thread.author: codex
thread.body: Added examples: a tiny starter-deck Cultist fight for basic UI/search plumbing, a captured Act 1 trace combat for real-state comparison, and a hard late-Act trace combat for testing whether advice helps trace collection.

anchor.quote: replace the current trace UI with this UI; reuse only bridge plumbing that is
anchor.originalQuote: How much of the existing trace UI should be replaced versus reused as bridge

thread.author: user
thread.body: the current trace UI is pretty bad, easily gets out of sync etc. I think scrap the whole thing and replace it with this? WDYT? I'm thinking we will collect traces through this in the future.

thread.author: codex
thread.body: I agree. I updated the plan to replace the current trace UI with this new UI, reuse only bridge plumbing that remains reliable, and make the new bridge-control flow the future trace-collection path once sync is reliable enough.

anchor.quote: Which fidelity categories should be elevated into first-class UI badges?
anchor.originalQuote: Which fidelity categories should be elevated into first-class UI badges?

thread.author: codex
thread.body: Added concrete badge examples: source_backed for high-trust decoded/trace-backed behavior, captured_branch for one observed branch that is not generalized, placeholder/legacy_fixed for scaffolding that search should treat skeptically, and verifier_only for trace repair/comparison machinery that must not look like game mechanics.

anchor.quote: Should live bridge control send to the simulator first, the real game first,
anchor.originalQuote: Should live bridge control apply to simulator first, game first, or both with
anchor.suffix: or both as one coordinated operation? Examples:

thread.author: codex
thread.body: Added examples for the three possible bridge-control orders: simulator-first protects the real run from invalid UI actions, game-first treats the real game as source of truth, and coordinated both-mode records predicted simulator state plus observed game state and surfaces parity diffs.
